data.title: CFTF Investigating Pathways to Allow U.S. Traders Access to Offshore Crypto Exchanges
data.description:
The Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) is actively conducting a thorough investigation into potential pathways that would enable traders in the United States to gain access to offshore cryptocurrency exchanges, which are often perceived as more flexible and innovative platforms for trading digital assets. This exploration seeks to understand the regulatory and operational frameworks that could facilitate such access while ensuring compliance with U.S. laws and protecting investors in the rapidly evolving landscape of cryptocurrency trading.
source: https://blocknews.com/cftf-investigating-pathways-to-allow-u-s-traders-access-to-offshore-crypto-exchanges/

data.title: Canada’s current account deficit blows past records amid trade war
data.description:
Canada’s external accounts swung to a new low in the second quarter as sales to the United States shrank during the trade fight, pushing the country’s current account shortfall to a record. Statistics Canada said Thursday the deficit widened to C$21. 16 billion ($15. 4 billion) in the April–June period. That is the biggest gap since at […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/canada-us-trade-fight-drives-deficit/

data.title: BNB Sees Brief Surge Amid CFTC's Upcoming Guidance
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, BNB experienced a brief increase of over 1.5%, with its price temporarily reaching $875.

Previously, it was reported that crypto journalist Eleanor Terrett stated that the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) is set to release guidance clarifying its Foreign Board of Trade (FBOT) registration rules. This guidance aims to provide a legal pathway for non-U.S. trading platforms to allow U.S. users to trade on their platforms.

data.title: CFTC to Clarify Foreign Trading Platform Registration Rules
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) is set to release guidance on the registration rules for Foreign Board of Trade (FBOT) platforms. This move aims to provide a legal pathway for non-U.S. trading platforms to allow American users to trade on their platforms.

Acting Chair Caroline D. Pham stated that this initiative seeks to bring crypto activities back to the United States, which had previously moved abroad due to regulatory enforcement during the Biden administration. She emphasized the longstanding regulatory framework that has been in place since the 1990s.

For U.S. traders, this development means legal access to more global liquidity. For the crypto industry, it represents another step towards regulatory clarity and aligns with the Trump administration's "crypto sprint" strategy.

data.title: US government taps Pyth, Chainlink among blockchains to process economic data on-chain
data.description:
The U. S. Department of Commerce has announced partnerships with blockchain oracle providers Chainlink and Pyth Network to publish official macroeconomic data on-chain, marking a significant milestone in integrating blockchain technology with government data infrastructure. The initiative aims to enhance the transparency and accessibility of economic data by leveraging decentralized networks
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/us-pyth-chainlink-to-process-economic-data/
